public.title: CityMind AI
public.subtitle: Clear reports for officers who decide — AI only helps organize the facts.
public.description: Empowering citizens and city officials to collaborate on urban incident reports to improve municipal services.
public.heroAdvisory: AI helps organize reports. Officers review and decide.
public.floatingBarLabel: Report a neighborhood issue on your terms
public.howItWorksDesc: Describe a community issue once. CityMind organizes the details so officers can review and decide what happens next.
public.instructionsDesc: Five short steps from description to a saveable report ID and access token.
public.step1Desc: Type a clear description of the urban incident you encountered.
public.step2Desc: Location helps officers respond faster. You can still submit without it.
public.step3Desc: Optional photo — JPEG, PNG, or WebP. Keep it under the size limit shown.
public.step4Desc: Send your report for AI-assisted organization. Officers remain the decision authority.
public.step5Title: Keep your ID and access token
public.step5Desc: Save your report ID and access token. You’ll need them to check status later.
public.aboutHeadline: A modern civic desk for clearer reports and evidence-based decisions
public.aboutDesc: CityMind helps communities turn scattered incident reports into structured, auditable insights officers can act on.
public.missionDesc: Make it simple for anyone to report a neighborhood issue and trust that officers see clear evidence.
public.visionDesc: AI structures and prioritizes. Officers verify and decide. No autonomous authority.
public.locationHelper: Location helps officers respond faster. You can still submit without it.
public.imageHelper: Optional photo — JPEG, PNG, or WebP. Keep it under the size limit shown.
public.formErrorClient: Fix the highlighted fields, then try again.
public.formErrorNetwork: Could not send your report. Check your connection and try again.
public.successBody: Your report is saved. Review the AI guidance below, then keep your ID and token to check status later.

successOutcome.title: What we found
successOutcome.advisoryNote: Advisory only — a city officer makes the final decision.
successOutcome.summaryFallback: Your report was reviewed. Follow the recommended steps below.
successOutcome.selfHelpNext: You can try the safe steps below on your own. Escalate to the city anytime if the issue persists.
successOutcome.governmentNext: Your report is in the officer review queue. Track progress with your report ID and access token.

public.tokenWarning: This token is shown once. We can’t show it again.
public.statusLinkPrep: Status link — copy to check updates
public.statusBody: Enter your report ID and access token to see updates.
public.statusPrivacyNote: Your status link is private. Don’t share it publicly.
public.statusEmptyHeading: Enter your details to check status
public.statusEmptyBody: Use the report ID and access token from your submission confirmation.
public.statusVerifyFailed: We couldn’t verify that report and token. Check both and try again.
public.statusRateLimited: Too many attempts — try again shortly.
public.statusNetworkError: Could not check status. Check your connection and try again.

stats.sectionBody: Recent public totals only — no personal details.

map.label: Pin the location (optional)
map.helper: Tap the map to place a pin. You can still submit without it.
map.degraded: Map unavailable — enter coordinates or use your location.

routing.playbookFallbackBody: Step-by-step guidance is being prepared. You can still ask the city for help below.
routing.escalateHelper: An officer can review your report and follow up.
routing.escalateConfirmBody: Your report will join the officer review queue. You can keep using the same report ID and access token.
routing.escalateSuccess: Your report was sent to the city for review.
routing.escalateError: Could not send your report to the city. Check your connection and try again.
routing.escalatedNotice: The city is now reviewing your report.

pothole.title: Small pothole — what you can do
steps: Mark the area with cones or chalk so neighbors can see it.
Take a photo from a safe distance if conditions change.
Check your city's road repair request page for DIY patch kits in some areas.
If the hole widens or affects traffic, use the button below to send it to officers.

waste.title: Missed or overflowing waste — next steps
steps: Confirm your collection day on the city waste schedule.
Place bins at the curb by the posted time with lids closed.
Report missed pickup to the city waste line if it is the day after collection.
For illegal dumping, note the location and avoid touching hazardous items.

streetlight.title: Streetlight out — quick checks
steps: Note the pole number or nearest address if visible.
Check whether nearby lights are also out (possible circuit issue).
Avoid climbing poles or opening equipment — leave repairs to the utility.
If the area feels unsafe at night, escalate to city officers below.

graffiti.title: Graffiti on public property — guidance
steps: Photograph the tagging from a safe distance for your records.
Do not confront anyone you see tagging — contact police if activity is ongoing.
Many cities offer free graffiti removal kits for small tags on your own fence.
For public walls or recurring tags, city crews may need to schedule removal.

triage.calmNoticeBody: Automated review is unavailable. Your report is saved and will be reviewed by an officer.

coach.governmentPathBody: Your report is queued for officer review.

intake.title: Report with chat support
intake.subtitle: Describe your issue in conversation. Submit when you're ready — an officer still reviews every report.
intake.welcome: Hi! I'm here to help you report a community issue. Tell me what happened, where it is, and share a photo if you have one.

dashboard.title: CityMind AI Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Decision support for recent citizen reports. Officers make final decisions.
dashboard.pageSubtitle: Filter, review, and export citizen reports. AI suggestions stay advisory — you decide.
